content: In regular rust one can return Result from a doctest, but that is not supported in rust-for-linux. Is anybody planning on adding support for returning Result from doctest?

What we do is wrap the test into a block like:

/// ```
/// # use kernel::{spawn_work_item, workqueue};
///
/// # fn example() -> Result {
/// spawn_work_item!(workqueue::system(), || pr_info!("Hello from a work item\n"))?;
/// #     Ok(())
/// # }
///
/// # example().unwrap()
/// ```

There is nowadays support in rustdoc to do this automatically for userspace tests, and I plan to have something similar too. For the moment, please write them like that, and I will clean them up when we have support for it all at once.
(Edited the comment above to give a better example that actually hides the wrapping function)
rustdoc already outputs something like the above when a doctest returns a Result. I was thinking if we wrap the output of rustdoc in a function instead of extracting the body, it would just work

Yeah, definitely, that is what I meant by rustdoc having support for it. I think we can still extract the body, i.e. it is orthogonal (IIRC I did it because I had to extract the crate-level attributes anyway).
I will give it a look later today.
